title: Negotiations with Bay Area Legislators and Governor’s Office Lead to Increased Funds for Vaccine Distribution to Integrate Hardest-Hit Communities in the Bay Area
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Approximately $7,600,000 will be allocated to the nine Bay Area counties for administering vaccines in communities at increased risk of COVID-19 infection and hospitalization.",
    "The Bay Area accounts for 20% of the population of California but only 2% of Bay Area residents fell under zip codes prioritized for nearly half of the available COVID-19 vaccine doses.",
    "The vaccine distribution formula excluded Bay Area zip codes that have COVID-19 infection rates nearly twice that of the state's overall rate.",
    "Funding can be used for expanding vaccination sites in hard-hit areas and hiring workers for culturally competent outreach.",
    "Each local health jurisdiction will deploy funding to accelerate vaccines to vulnerable, high-risk populations.",
    "The state is expecting an increase of 4,000,000 vaccine doses per week from the federal government by mid-May.",
    "The COVID-19 pandemic has exacerbated health and economic disparities, with underserved communities experiencing the brunt of job loss and health impacts.",
    "Only an equitable vaccination rollout will ensure life-saving treatment reaches those who need it."
  ]
}
```
